body:
### Problem

I started with the one-shot OptionSet autocommand to notice (on init) if both `bg` abd `tgc` has been set (to select colorscheme looking good for these settings) and noticed that after returning `true` by attached callback that autocommand still exist, but only for the pattern not triggered the callback. To delete the autocommand we need to fire events matching to all patterns. 

### Steps to reproduce

init.lua: 
```lua
vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('User', {
  pattern = 'Event1,Event2',
  callback = function (data)
    print(('%s has been fired.'):format(data.match))
    return true
  end,
})

function Trigger(event)
  vim.api.nvim_exec_autocmds('User', {pattern = event})
  vim.schedule(function ()
    vim.print(vim.api.nvim_get_autocmds({event = 'User'}))
  end)
end

Trigger('Event1')
```
```
nvim --clean -u init.lua
```
- look into `:messages`
- call `:lua Trigger('Event2')`
- look into `:messages` again

I see 
![image](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/assets/11404453/56150b6b-258c-4b1e-bee5-b8afab578356)


### Expected behavior

If it's intended, please update documentation, otherwise it needs to be fixed (i.e. returning `true` should delete the whole autocommand). 

### Neovim version (nvim -v)

v0.10.0-dev-1802+g29aa4dd10

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

-

### Operating system/version

Debian Sid

### Terminal name/version

kitty 0.31.0, tmux 3.3a-5

### $TERM environment variable

tmux-256color

### Installation

from repo

comment_data.body:
> (i.e. returning true should delete the whole autocommand)

Well `autocmd-pattern` says that there are 2 separate autocmds created.
```
The {aupat} argument of `:autocmd` can be a comma-separated list.  This works as
if the command was given with each pattern separately.  Thus this command:
	:autocmd BufRead *.txt,*.info set et
Is equivalent to:
	:autocmd BufRead *.txt set et
	:autocmd BufRead *.info set et
```
I wouldn't except 2 separate autocmds to lose their one-shot if only 1 lost it.

If we leave such autocommand untouched, then `=vim.print(vim.api.nvim_get_autocmds({event = 'User'}))` shows (indeed) two autocommands

```lua
{ {                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
    buflocal = false,                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    callback = <function 1>,                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
    command = "",                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
    event = "User",                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
    id = 6,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    once = false,                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
    pattern = "Event1"                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
  }, {                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
    buflocal = false,                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    callback = <function 1>,                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
    command = "",                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
    event = "User",                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
    id = 6,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    once = false,                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
    pattern = "Event2"                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
  } }                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
{ {                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
    buflocal = false,                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    callback = <function 1>,                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
    command = "",                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
    event = "User",                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
    id = 6,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    once = false,                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
    pattern = "Event1"                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
  }, {                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
    buflocal = false,                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    callback = <function 1>,                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
    command = "",                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
    event = "User",                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
    id = 6,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    once = false,                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
    pattern = "Event2"                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
  } } 
```
 although with the same `id` value.

You could use that with `nvim_del_autocmd`.
`nvim_create_autocmd` returns it.
I guess they weren't so separate after all.

i think there should be more documentation on autocmd ids
```
    Return:  
        Autocommand id (number)
    NOTE: an autocmd id can refer to multiple autocmds (i.e. multi-pattern autocmds see |autocmd-pattern|)
```
Do you have a better idea of what to write?

Sorry with my earlier response i wasn't aware of the exists of autocmd ids.

I rather have no problem with workaround/resolve it on the user side but have doubt how it should work (including indexing) on the Lua API side and be described in `:help nvim_create_autocmd`. Let's wait for more maintainers to take a look at this issue.
`pattern = 'Event1,Event2'` creates 2 handlers as noted, but sharing the same `id`. Hmm.

labels.description: Need a decision with a clear, actionable path.
labels.name: needs:decision

milestone.description: Low priority items. We plan to work on this but don't have a target date.
milestone.title: backlog

title: events: `pattern='Event1,Event2'` creates 2 handlers sharing the same id (and `return true` deletes only 1 handler)
